course_description: Sân bay & an ninh, nhập cảnh & hải quan, khách sạn, giao thông, nhà hàng, tham quan & chụp ảnh, mua sắm, khẩn cấp, kết bạn trên đường, đọc/viết review du lịch.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Check-in terminology
theory_pages.body:
### Check-in terminology

| Từ | Nghĩa |
|----|--------|
| **Terminal** | nhà ga (quốc tế / nội địa) |
| **Boarding pass** | thẻ lên máy bay |
| **Checked baggage** | hành lý ký gửi |
| **Carry-on luggage** | hành lý xách tay |
| **Overweight** | quá cân (hành lý) |
| **Gate** | cửa khởi hành |

**Ngữ cảnh:** *Your flight departs from Gate 12, Terminal 2.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Dialogue & seats
theory_pages.body:
### Checking in & seats

**Nhân viên:** *May I have your passport and booking reference, please?*

**Khách:** *I'd like a window seat / an aisle seat, please.*

**Hành lý:** *Is my luggage checked through to the final destination?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Security & batteries
theory_pages.body:
### Security & special gear

Trình tự: *empty your pockets* → *remove your belt* → *electronics in the bin*.

*Nguồn:* *I have sensitive photography equipment. Can I have a manual inspection?*

**Pin:** *Lithium batteries must be in carry-on luggage.*

content.reading_passage: Your flight departs from Gate 12, Terminal 2. Please empty your pockets before security. Put laptops in the bin. Carry-on only for lithium batteries.

vocabulary.vi: thẻ lên máy / cửa / ghế lối đi

examples.english: I'd like an aisle seat, please.

quizzes.question_text: Thẻ dùng để lên máy bay gọi là:
Bạn muốn ngồi gần lối đi (dễ đứng dậy), bạn chọn:
My suitcase is 25 kg. The limit is 20 kg. It is _____.
Find the problem: “I have a luggage.”
Ghép hợp lý: 1. Carry-on — 2. Checked
quizzes.hint_text: Xách tay nhỏ; ký gửi lớn.

quizzes.question_text: At security, laptops and cameras usually go:
Lithium batteries are allowed in checked baggage.
“Cửa khởi hành số mấy?” — Choose the best English:

options.A: Which gate is it? / What is the gate number?

quizzes.question_text: You carry a mirrorless camera and lenses on the plane. They should go in:
Please _____ your pockets and remove your shoes.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Immigration
theory_pages.body:
### Immigration — short, honest answers

- *Purpose of visit:* *I'm here for vacation / business.*  
- *Duration:* *I'm staying for 10 days.* / *I'm going to stay for two weeks.*  
- *Accommodation:* *I'm staying at the Grand Hotel.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Customs
theory_pages.body:
### Customs

- *declare*, *prohibited items*, *duty-free*  
- *Do you have anything to declare?* — *Nothing to declare.*  
- **Green channel** — nothing to declare; **red channel** — goods to declare.

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Be going to
theory_pages.body:
### Grammar: **be going to** (travel plans)

*I am going to visit Kyoto.*  
*We are going to stay in a traditional house.*

content.reading_passage: Officer: What's the purpose of your visit? — Traveler: I'm here for vacation. I'm going to stay for 10 days at a hotel. Do you have anything to declare? — Nothing to declare.

examples.english: I am going to stay at this hotel for 5 days.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi định ở lại khách sạn này 5 ngày.

quizzes.question_text: Officer: “What's the purpose of your visit?”
How long are you _____ to stay? (plan)
You have nothing to pay tax on. You use:
quizzes.hint_text: Green channel when nothing to declare.

quizzes.question_text: Better for a **planned** stay length: “I ___ for two weeks.”
“Duty-free” means you don't pay tax on those goods (in that shop/zone).
Match: 1. Vacation — 2. Business
“Tôi định ở lại khách sạn này trong 5 ngày.”

options.A: I am going to stay at this hotel for 5 days.
options.B: I going to stay at this hotel.
options.C: I am stay at this hotel for 5 days.

quizzes.question_text: Do you have a _____ ticket? (khứ hồi)
If they say “Show me your documents”, you typically show:
“Prohibited” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Rooms & issues
theory_pages.body:
### Check-in & room types

*Reservation*, *single / double / twin room*, *amenities* (WiFi, gym, pool)

**Vấn đề:** *The air conditioning is not working.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Services
theory_pages.body:
### Services

*Wake-up call*, *room service*, *housekeeping*

*Could I have some extra towels, please?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Check-out
theory_pages.body:
### Check-out & storage

*Check-out time*, *deposit*

*Can I leave my luggage here until 5 PM?*

content.reading_passage: I have a reservation under the name Smith. Twin room, please. Could I have extra towels? What is the WiFi password?

vocabulary.vi: phòng hai giường / báo thức / dọn phòng

examples.english: Can I leave my luggage here?
examples.vietnamese: Tôi có thể gửi hành lý ở đây không?

quizzes.question_text: Two people want **two separate beds:**
I have a _____ under the name “Phat”.
The AC is broken. You tell reception:
Fix: “I want a wake-up calling at 7 AM.”
Match: 1. Room service — 2. Housekeeping
You usually pay the full “deposit” when you check out only.
“Tôi có thể gửi hành lý ở đây không?”

options.A: Can I leave my luggage here?
options.B: Can I live my luggage here?

quizzes.question_text: What is the _____ password?
Breakfast in your room? Call:
“Complimentary breakfast” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Public transport
theory_pages.body:
### Public transport

*Subway / metro / underground*, *fare*, *one-way / round-trip (return) ticket*, *line*, *platform*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Navigation
theory_pages.body:
### Navigation

*GPS*, *compass*, *directions* (*turn left/right*, *go straight*)

*Which line goes to the city center?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Apps & tech
theory_pages.body:
### Apps & tickets

*Ride-sharing* (Uber, Grab), *e-ticket*, *QR code*, *charging station*

content.reading_passage: Take Line 4 to Central Station. Buy a round-trip ticket. Scan your QR code at the gate. My phone died — I need a charging station.

vocabulary.vi: giá vé / sân ga / khứ hồi

examples.english: Which line goes to the museum?
examples.vietnamese: Tuyến nào đi đến bảo tàng?

quizzes.question_text: The price you pay for a train ride is often called the:
Take _____ 4 to the Central Station.
You go and come back on one purchase:
More natural when you are lost in a city:

options.B: Where is the nearest subway station?

quizzes.question_text: Match: 1. Turn left — 2. Go straight
A platform is where passengers wait for the train.
“Tuyến nào đi đến bảo tàng?”

options.A: Which line goes to the museum?

quizzes.question_text: I need to scan my _____ code to enter.
Phone out of battery while using maps — find a:
“Miss the train” means:

options.A: Arrive too late / lose the train

quizzes.hint_text: Miss = don't catch it in time.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Booking & allergies
theory_pages.body:
### Booking & ordering

*A table for [number]*, *menu*, *specialty*, *allergy* — *I'm allergic to peanuts.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Etiquette
theory_pages.body:
### At the table

*Napkin*, *cutlery* (fork, knife, spoon, chopsticks), *still / sparkling water*

*Could we have the menu, please?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Payment
theory_pages.body:
### Paying

*Bill / check*, *service charge*, *split the bill*

*Do you accept credit cards?*

content.reading_passage: A table for four, please. I'm allergic to shellfish. Could I have the bill? Do you accept cards? Can we split the bill?

vocabulary.vi: dị ứng / món đặc sản / chia hóa đơn

examples.english: Could I have the bill, please?

quizzes.question_text: You enter with four people:
I am _____ to seafood.
You want sparkling water:
Politer than “Give me the bill.”:

options.B: Could I have the bill, please?

quizzes.question_text: Match: 1. Fork — 2. Chopsticks
“Specialty” is always a dish you find everywhere with no local link.
“Bạn có nhận thẻ tín dụng không?”
Can we _____ the bill?
You want the best dish here. You ask:

options.B: What is the specialty / What do you recommend?

quizzes.question_text: “Keep the change” usually means:

options.A: The server keeps extra money as a tip

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Signs & rules
theory_pages.body:
### Pre-reading — rules

*No flash photography*, *no tripods*, *admission fee*, *guided tour*

Đọc biển báo trước khi chụp — đặc biệt trong bảo tàng.

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Bài đọc
theory_pages.body:
### Reading — The Golden Hour in Prague

The text links **golden hour** light, **museum rules**, and **tripod permits**.

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Mô tả ảnh
theory_pages.body:
### Describing photos

*vibrant colors*, *breathtaking view*, *hidden gem*

content.reading_passage: Prague is a paradise for photographers. To capture the best images of the Charles Bridge, you should arrive at 'the golden hour' — just before sunset. The light is soft and orange. However, remember that some museums in the city center have strict rules. You can take photos, but 'No Flash' is a common rule to protect the old paintings. If you want to use a professional tripod, you might need a special permit. Always check the signs at the entrance before you take your Sony a6400 out of the bag.

vocabulary.vi: giờ vàng chụp ảnh / cấm đèn flash

quizzes.question_text: “The golden hour” is best for:
You can always use a tripod in every Prague museum without rules.
“No Flash” rules often protect old _____.
Where should you check rules first?
The camera model mentioned is Sony _____.
A “hidden gem” is usually:

options.A: A place many tourists never find / underrated spot

quizzes.question_text: The light at the golden hour is described as:
You might need a special permit to use a professional tripod in some places.
quizzes.hint_text: Text says you might need a permit.

quizzes.question_text: “Cảnh tượng thật tuyệt vời.” — natural English:
The text gives advice for _____ in Prague.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Prices & sizes
theory_pages.body:
### Prices & sizes

*How much is this?* / *How much does it cost?*

*Fitting room*, *S / M / L / XL*

*Do you have this in a larger size?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Bargaining
theory_pages.body:
### Bargaining

*bargain*, *pricey / expensive*, *a bit out of my budget*

*Could you give me a discount?* / *Is that your best price?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Refund
theory_pages.body:
### Refund & exchange

*receipt*, *refund*, *exchange*

*Can I exchange this if it doesn't fit?*

content.reading_passage: How much is this shirt? It's a bit out of my budget. Do you have a receipt? I need a refund.

vocabulary.vi: phòng thử / hóa đơn / đổi hàng

examples.vietnamese: Đây có phải giá tốt nhất chưa?

quizzes.question_text: Where you try on clothes:
Do you have this shirt in _____ (size vừa)?
Polite when something is too expensive:

options.B: It's a bit out of my budget.

quizzes.question_text: Fix: “I want a refunding.”
Match: 1. Receipt — 2. Discount
Heavy bargaining is usual in most big supermarkets.
“Đây có phải là giá tốt nhất của bạn chưa?”
I would like to _____ this for a smaller one.
You want souvenirs for friends. You look for a:
“Buy one get one free” means:

options.A: Buy one item, get another free

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Lost & theft
theory_pages.body:
### Lost items

*Lost and found*, *stolen*, *pickpocket*

*My wallet has been stolen!*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Medical
theory_pages.body:
### Medical

*pharmacy*, *prescription*, *emergency room*

*I need to see a doctor immediately.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Police & embassy
theory_pages.body:
### Police & embassy

*police station*, *report*, *embassy*

*I lost my passport. Where is the Vietnamese Embassy?*

content.reading_passage: I left my bag on the train. Go to Lost and Found. My wallet was stolen — report at the police station.

vocabulary.vi: bị trộm / móc túi / đại sứ quán

examples.english: I'm lost. Can you help me?
examples.vietnamese: Tôi bị lạc. Bạn giúp tôi được không?

quizzes.question_text: You forgot your bag on the train. First try:
Help! My bag has been _____.
Pickpockets are more common in:
More natural: “Where is the pharmacy store?”
Match: 1. Wallet — 2. Passport
You usually go to the embassy only because you lost your phone game.
“Tôi bị lạc đường. Bạn giúp tôi được không?”

options.A: I'm lost. Can you help me?
options.B: I lost. Can you help I?

quizzes.question_text: I have a severe headache. I need some _____.
Common emergency numbers in many countries (examples):
“Immediately” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Breaking the ice
theory_pages.body:
### Breaking the ice

*Is anyone sitting here?*

*Where are you from?* / *How long have you been here?*

*Do you travel alone or with friends?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Sharing
theory_pages.body:
### Sharing experiences

*What's your favorite place so far?*

*You should try the local coffee — it's amazing!*

*I'm a photographer — I love the architecture here.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Contacts
theory_pages.body:
### Staying in touch

*Are you on Instagram?*

*Let's exchange contacts.*

*Have a safe trip!*

content.reading_passage: Hi, is anyone sitting here? Where are you from? I'm from Vietnam. Let's exchange Instagram. Have a safe trip!

vocabulary.vi: cho đến nay / trao đổi liên lạc

examples.vietnamese: Chúc bạn chuyến đi an toàn!

quizzes.question_text: At a hostel, a friendly opener:
Is anyone _____ here?
Recommend a restaurant:

options.B: You should try that restaurant — the food is great.

quizzes.question_text: Better question about time since arrival:

options.B: How long have you been here?

quizzes.question_text: Match: 1. Solo traveler — 2. Group traveler
“Break the ice” means starting a conversation.
“Bạn có dùng Instagram không?”
Let's _____ contacts.
Saying goodbye to a new travel friend:

options.A: Have a safe trip! / Hope to see you again!

quizzes.question_text: In “What's your favorite place so far?”, “so far” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Review words
theory_pages.body:
### Review vocabulary

*highly recommend*, *overrated*, *value for money*, *disappointing*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Bài đọc
theory_pages.body:
### Reading — Singapore stay

Note: *value for money*, *overrated pool*, *Gardens by the Bay*, *breathtaking* light show.

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Cấu trúc viết review
theory_pages.body:
### Writing a review (structure)

Intro → **The good** → **The bad** → Conclusion (Would you go back?)

content.reading_passage: I recently stayed at a boutique hotel in Singapore. It was a great value for money. The location was perfect, right next to the MRT station. The staff were very helpful when I lost my camera's lens cap. However, the famous rooftop pool was a bit overrated because it was too crowded. If you are a photographer, I highly recommend visiting the Gardens by the Bay at night. The light show is breathtaking and perfect for long-exposure shots.

examples.vietnamese: Tôi chắc chắn sẽ quay lại.

quizzes.question_text: “Value for money” means:
The writer loved everything about the rooftop pool.
I highly _____ this hotel.
Why was the pool “overrated” for the writer?
Photographers should visit Gardens by the Bay at _____.
A “lens cap” belongs to a:
Which word in the text describes the light show most positively?
The hotel was far from the MRT station.
“Tôi chắc chắn sẽ quay lại.”
The text is a _____ of a travel experience.
